message:
fix(output): replace panics with graceful error handling in JSON/TOON serialization

- Replace all assert!/expect panics in json(), json_pretty(), toon(),
  and toon_with_stats() with graceful error reporting via new
  report_serialization_error() helper that prints to stderr in non-quiet
  mode and returns early instead of crashing
- Extract json_value() helper that returns Option<serde_json::Value>,
  encapsulating the serialize-or-report pattern used by rich JSON,
  TOON, and TOON-with-stats output paths
- Change pretty_json_len() from returning usize to Option<usize>,
  removing the expect() call and letting callers handle None gracefully
- Simplify toon_with_stats() stats emission: use if-let on json_chars
  Option instead of expect() + separate bool guard
- Add FailingSerialize test helper and json_value_returns_none_on_serialize_error
  test validating the new error-returns-None behavior
- Update pretty_json_len test to expect Some(len)

These changes prevent br from panicking on unusual serialization inputs
(e.g., non-string map keys, recursive structures, or custom Serialize
impls that fail) — instead printing a clear error and continuing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
